name: oslo-log

metadata.name: oslo.log
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# Team and repository tags

```{image} https://governance.openstack.org/tc/badges/oslo.log.svg
:target: https://governance.openstack.org/tc/reference/tags/index.html
```

% Change things from this point on

# oslo.log -- Oslo Logging Library

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/oslo.log.svg
:alt: Latest Version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/oslo.log/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/oslo.log.svg
:alt: Downloads
:target: https://pypi.org/project/oslo.log/
```

The oslo.log (logging) configuration library provides standardized
configuration for all openstack projects. It also provides custom
formatters, handlers and support for context specific
logging (like resource id's etc).

- Free software: Apache license
- Documentation: <https://docs.openstack.org/oslo.log/latest/>
- Source: <https://opendev.org/openstack/oslo.log>
- Bugs: <https://bugs.launchpad.net/oslo.log>
- Release notes: <https://docs.openstack.org/releasenotes/oslo.log/>

functions.name: oslo_log.helpers._is_method
documentation:
Returns True if a given method is obj's method.

You can not simply test a given method like:

return inspect.ismethod(method)

This is because functools.wraps converts the method to a function
in log_method_call function.

functions.name: oslo_log.helpers.log_method_call
documentation:
Decorator helping to log method calls.

```
param method

Method to decorate to be logged.

type method

method definition
```

functions.name: oslo_log.pipe_mutex.pipe_createLock
documentation: Replacement for logging.Handler.createLock method.

classes.name: oslo_log.pipe_mutex.PipeMutex

methods.name: oslo_log.pipe_mutex.PipeMutex.acquire
documentation:
Acquire the mutex.

If called with blocking=False, returns True if the mutex was
acquired and False if it wasn't. Otherwise, blocks until the mutex
is acquired and returns True.
This lock is recursive; the same greenthread may acquire it as many
times as it wants to, though it must then release it that many times
too.

methods.name: oslo_log.pipe_mutex.PipeMutex.release
documentation: Release the mutex.

methods.name: oslo_log.pipe_mutex.PipeMutex.close
documentation:
Close the mutex.

This releases its file descriptors.
You can't use a mutex after it's been closed.

Mutex using a pipe.

Works across both greenlets and real threads, even at the same time.

Class code copied from Swift's swift/common/utils.py
Related eventlet bug: https://github.com/eventlet/eventlet/issues/432

classes.name: oslo_log.fixture.setlevel.SetLogLevel
documentation:
Override the log level for the named loggers, restoring their
previous value at the end of the test.

To use:

```
from oslo_log import fixture as log_fixture

self.useFixture(log_fixture.SetLogLevel(['myapp.foo'], logging.DEBUG))
```

```
param logger_names

Sequence of logger names, as would be passed
to getLogger().

type logger_names

list(str)

param level

Logging level, usually one of logging.DEBUG,
logging.INFO, etc.

type level

int
```

functions.name: oslo_log.rate_limit._iter_loggers
documentation: Iterate on existing loggers.

functions.name: oslo_log.rate_limit.install_filter
documentation:
Install a rate limit filter on existing and future loggers.

Limit logs to *burst* messages every *interval* seconds, except of levels
>= *except_level*. *except_level* is a log level name like 'CRITICAL'. If
*except_level* is an empty string, all levels are filtered.

The filter uses a monotonic clock, the timestamp of log records is not
used.

Raise an exception if a rate limit filter is already installed.

functions.name: oslo_log.rate_limit.uninstall_filter
documentation:
Uninstall the rate filter installed by install_filter().

Do nothing if the filter was already uninstalled.

functions.name: oslo_log._options.list_opts
documentation:
Returns a list of oslo.config options available in the library.

The returned list includes all oslo.config options which may be registered
at runtime by the library.

Each element of the list is a tuple. The first element is the name of the
group under which the list of elements in the second element will be
registered. A group name of None corresponds to the [DEFAULT] group in
config files.

The purpose of this is to allow tools like the Oslo sample config file
generator (oslo-config-generator) to discover the options exposed to users
by this library.

:returns: a list of (group_name, opts) tuples

functions.name: oslo_log.formatters._update_record_with_context
documentation:
Given a log record, update it with context information.

The request context, if there is one, will either be passed with the
incoming record or in the global thread-local store.

functions.name: oslo_log.formatters._ensure_unicode
documentation: Do our best to turn the input argument into a unicode object.

functions.name: oslo_log.formatters._get_error_summary
documentation:
Return the error summary

If there is no active exception, return the default.

If the record is being logged below the warning level, return an
empty string.

If there is an active exception, format it and return the
resulting string.

It expects to be used by fluent.handler.FluentHandler.

documentation:
Initialize ContextFormatter instance

Takes additional keyword arguments which can be used in the message
format string.

```
keyword project

project name

type project

string

keyword version

project version

type version

string
```

methods.name: oslo_log.formatters.ContextFormatter.format
documentation: Uses contextstring if request_id is set, otherwise default.

methods.name: oslo_log.formatters.ContextFormatter.formatException
documentation: Format exception output with CONF.logging_exception_prefix.

A context.RequestContext aware formatter configured through flags.

The flags used to set format strings are: logging_context_format_string
and logging_default_format_string.  You can also specify
logging_debug_format_suffix to append extra formatting if the log level is
debug.

The standard variables available to the formatter are listed at:
http://docs.python.org/library/logging.html#formatter

In addition to the standard variables, one custom variable is
available to both formatting string: `isotime` produces a
timestamp in ISO8601 format, suitable for producing
RFC5424-compliant log messages.

Furthermore, logging_context_format_string has access to all of
the data in a dict representation of the context.

documentation:
Log handler that sets the 'color' key based on the level

To use, include a '%(color)s' entry in the logging_context_format_string.
There is also a '%(reset_color)s' key that can be used to manually reset
the color within a log line.

functions.name: oslo_log.versionutils.deprecation_warning
documentation:
Warn about the deprecation of a feature.

```
param what

name of the thing being deprecated.

param as_of

the release deprecating the callable.

param in_favor_of

the replacement for the callable (optional)

param remove_in

an integer specifying how many releases to wait
before removing (default: 2)

param logger

the logging object to use for reporting (optional).
```

functions.name: oslo_log.versionutils.report_deprecated_feature
documentation:
Call this function when a deprecated feature is used.

If the system is configured for fatal deprecations then the message
is logged at the 'critical' level and {class}`DeprecatedConfig` will
be raised.

Otherwise, the message will be logged (once) at the 'warn' level.

```
raises

 if the system is configured for
fatal deprecations.
```

classes.name: oslo_log.versionutils.deprecated

methods.name: oslo_log.versionutils.deprecated.__init__
documentation:
Initialize decorator

```
param as_of

the release deprecating the callable. Constants
are define in this class for convenience.

param in_favor_of

the replacement for the callable (optional)

param remove_in

an integer specifying how many releases to wait
before removing (default: 2)

param what

name of the thing being deprecated (default: the
callable's name)
```

A decorator to mark callables as deprecated.

This decorator logs a deprecation message when the callable it decorates is
used. The message will include the release where the callable was
deprecated, the release where it may be removed and possibly an optional
replacement. It also logs a message when a deprecated exception is being
caught in a try-except block, but not when subclasses of that exception
are being caught.

Examples:

1. Specifying the required deprecated release

```pycon
>>> @deprecated(as_of=deprecated.ICEHOUSE)
... def a(): pass
```

2. Specifying a replacement:

```pycon
>>> @deprecated(as_of=deprecated.ICEHOUSE, in_favor_of='f()')
... def b(): pass
```

3. Specifying the release where the functionality may be removed:

```pycon
>>> @deprecated(as_of=deprecated.ICEHOUSE, remove_in=+1)
... def c(): pass
```

4. Specifying the deprecated functionality will not be removed:

```pycon
>>> @deprecated(as_of=deprecated.ICEHOUSE, remove_in=None)
... def d(): pass
```

5. Specifying a replacement, deprecated functionality will not be removed:

```pycon
>>> @deprecated(as_of=deprecated.ICEHOUSE, in_favor_of='f()',
...             remove_in=None)
... def e(): pass
```

:::{warning}
The hook used to detect when a deprecated exception is being
*caught* does not work under Python 3. Deprecated exceptions
are still logged if they are thrown.
:::

modules.name: oslo_log.log
documentation:
OpenStack logging handler.

This module adds to logging functionality by adding the option to specify
a context object when calling the various log methods.  If the context object
is not specified, default formatting is used. Additionally, an instance uuid
may be passed as part of the log message, which is intended to make it easier
for admins to find messages related to a specific instance.

It also allows setting of formatting information through conf.

documentation:

Workaround for: https://github.com/eventlet/eventlet/issues/432

documentation: Setup logging for the current application.

functions.name: oslo_log.log.set_defaults
documentation: Set default values for the configuration options used by oslo.log.

functions.name: oslo_log.log.tempest_set_log_file
documentation:
Provide an API for tempest to set the logging filename.

:::{warning}
Only Tempest should use this function.
:::

We don't want applications to set a default log file, so we don't
want this in set_defaults(). Because tempest doesn't use a
configuration file we don't have another convenient way to safely
set the log file default.

functions.name: oslo_log.log._refresh_root_level
documentation:
Set the level of the root logger.

```
param debug

If 'debug' is True, the level will be DEBUG.
Otherwise the level will be INFO.
```

documentation: Return a copy of the oslo loggers dictionary.

functions.name: oslo_log.log.getLogger
documentation:
Build a logger with the given name.

```
param name

The name for the logger. This is usually the module
name, __name__.

type name

string

param project

The name of the project, to be injected into log
messages. For example, 'nova'.

type project

string

param version

The version of the project, to be injected into log
messages. For example, '2014.2'.

type version

string
```

functions.name: oslo_log.log.get_default_log_levels
documentation:
Return the Oslo Logging default log levels.

Returns a copy of the list so an application can change the value
and not affect the default value used in the log_opts configuration
setup.

functions.name: oslo_log.log.is_debug_enabled
documentation: Determine if debug logging mode is enabled.

classes.name: oslo_log.log.KeywordArgumentAdapter
documentation:
Logger adapter to add keyword arguments to log record's extra data

Keywords passed to the log call are added to the "extra"
dictionary passed to the underlying logger so they are emitted
with the log message and available to the format string.

Special keywords:

extra

: An existing dictionary of extra values to be passed to the
  logger. If present, the dictionary is copied and extended.

resource
: A dictionary-like object containing a `name` key or `type`

  : and `id` keys.

classes.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.CommonLoggerTestsMixIn
documentation:
These tests are shared between LoggerTestCase and
LazyLoggerTestCase.

classes.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.LogTestBase

methods.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.LogTestBase._add_handler_with_cleanup
documentation:
Add a log handler to a log instance.

This function should be used to add handlers to loggers in test cases
instead of directly adding them to ensure that the handler is
correctly removed at the end of the test.  Otherwise the handler may
be left on the logger and interfere with subsequent tests.

```
param log_instance

The log instance to which the handler will be
added.

param handler

The handler class to be added.  Must be the class
itself, not an instance.

param formatter

The formatter class to set on the handler.  Must be
the class itself, not an instance.
```

methods.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.LogTestBase._set_log_level_with_cleanup
documentation:
Set the log level of a logger for the duration of a test.

Use this function to set the log level of a logger and add the
necessary cleanup to reset it back to default at the end of the test.

```
param log_instance

The logger whose level will be changed.

param level

The new log level to use.
```
Base test class that provides some convenience functions.

classes.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.SysLogHandlersTestCase

methods.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.SysLogHandlersTestCase.test_standard_format
documentation: Ensure syslog msg isn't modified for standard handler.
Test the standard Syslog handler.

classes.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.OSJournalHandlerTestCase
documentation:
Test systemd journal logging.

This is a lightweight test for testing systemd journal logging. It
mocks out the journal interface itself, which allows us to not
have to have systemd-python installed (which is not possible to
install on non Linux environments).

Real world testing is also encouraged.

classes.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.DictStreamHandler
documentation:
Serialize dict in order to avoid TypeError in python 3. It is needed for
FluentFormatterTestCase.

classes.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.ExceptionLoggingTestCase
documentation: Test that Exceptions are logged.

classes.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.FancyRecordTestCase
documentation:
Test how we handle fancy record keys that are not in the
base python logging.

methods.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.MutateTestCase.mk_log_config
documentation:
Turns a dictConfig-like structure into one suitable for fileConfig.

The schema is not validated as this is a test helper not production
code. Garbage in, garbage out. Particularly, don't try to use filters,
fileConfig doesn't support them.

Handler args must be passed like 'args': (1, 2). dictConfig passes
keys by keyword name and fileConfig passes them by position so
accepting the dictConfig form makes it nigh impossible to produce the
fileConfig form.

I traverse dicts by sorted keys for output stability but it doesn't
matter if defaulted keys are out of order.

methods.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.MutateTestCase.test_mk_log_config_empty
documentation: Ensure mk_log_config tolerates missing bits

methods.name: oslo_log.tests.unit.test_log.LogConfigOptsTestCase.test_handlers_cleanup
documentation: Test that all old handlers get removed from log_root.
